text: This paper explains personal and communal attitudes of Maduranese toward carok and the reasons of existing carok. Descriptive research was used in this study by involving 1195 Maduranese. Data collection was carried out by using observation, questionnaire, and interview methods. Based on the analysis of the questionnaire, there were four personal attitudes toward carok. They were 1) they did not intent to carry out carok (75%), 2) they would never carry out carok in any circumstanced (60%), 3) they would solve all situations wisely without carrying out carok (77.38%), and 4) they carried out carok not because of being obedient to State and Religious laws (77.40%). On the other hand, five communal attitudes toward carok involving 1) carok was not Madura tradition (64.16%), 2) Madura community loved peace (81.11%), 3) carok did not represent Maduranese (86.11%), 4) carok was a bad system (82.44%), and 5) they solved all situations wisely without carrying out carok (76.11%). In addition the motivations of carok were women, misunderstanding, the problem of the inheritance, belief, theft, and debt.
explanation: The text resembles a research abstract with concrete survey results and domain-specific terms, and while there are some awkward phrasings, the numerical data and context suggest human authorship rather than typical AI-generated content.
